subject: Chemistry
title: Lidocaine: Numbing Without Needles
description: One-minute skill: Lidocaine: Numbing Without Needles.

hook.text: Lidocaine blocks nerve signals so precisely that dentists can drill your tooth while you feel nothing. It was discovered in 1943.

buildup.text: Before lidocaine, cocaine was the local anesthetic of choice. It worked but was addictive and caused overdose deaths.

discovery.text: Lidocaine blocks sodium channels in nerve cells. Without sodium flow, nerves can't fire pain signals to the brain.

twist.text: Lidocaine also treats irregular heartbeats. The same sodium channels that carry pain signals also control heart rhythm.

climax.text: Lidocaine patches, creams, and injections are now standard. It's on the WHO's List of Essential Medicines.

punchline.text: Block sodium channels, block pain signals. One molecule silences nerves on command.

quiz.question: How does lidocaine prevent pain?
options: It blocks sodium channels in nerve cells, stopping pain signals from firing
It destroys pain receptors temporarily at the injection site
It floods the area with endorphins to override pain
